title: Jack Reacher
reviews: Cruise delivers surprisingly grounded performance here
Brutal action sequences feel authentically visceral
Character study disguised as thriller
Old school detective work refreshingly methodical
Violence has genuine weight and consequence
Perfectly paced investigation keeps you engaged
Reacher's intimidation factor absolutely works here
Solid adaptation respects source material well
Mystery unravels with satisfying logical precision
Cruise's physicality convinces despite height difference
Gritty tone elevates standard revenge plot
Supporting cast provides excellent dramatic foundation
Action choreography emphasizes brutality over spectacle
Werner Herzog's villain chills to bone
Investigation feels genuinely procedural and smart
Character's moral code drives compelling narrative
Surprisingly thoughtful approach to vigilante justice
Tension builds steadily without rushing payoffs
Realistic combat makes every fight meaningful
Exceeds expectations for franchise starter film
